title: relay ws.rs: bad system clock silently zeros PoW timestamp floor
description: relay/src/ws.rs:256-259: `SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).unwrap_or_default().as_secs()` silently returns 0 when the system clock is before the Unix epoch. Consequence: PoW staleness check uses ts_floor=0, which allows tokens up to 2^64-1 seconds in the future to appear valid — operator has no indication the clock is broken. core/src/transport.rs already uses `.context("system clock error")?` for the same pattern. Fix: apply the same pattern in ws.rs: propagate the error to the connection handler rather than silently defaulting.
